title: Sentences you already own
pedagogy: Statement patterns recombining units 1–10 vocabulary, nothing new taught. Patterns: (1) adjective follows noun with no copula — ข้าวอร่อยมาก (khao F: high ข + mai tho; aroi L·L; mak F: low ม + dead long), อันนี้แพงมาก (an M: mid อ + live น; ni H: LOW น + mai tho; phaeng M: low พ + live ง; mak F); (2) วัน + นี้ composes 'today' — วันนี้ร้อนมาก (wan M: low ว + live น; ni H; ron H: LOW ร + mai tho; mak F); (3) verb chaining ไป + กิน with no 'to'; (4) the ขอ + thing + หน่อย + particle request frame from food-03. Listening contrasts the openers กิน (mid) / หิว (rising) / ขอ (rising) that differ by consonant and tone, and the bare-feeling statement เหนื่อยมากค่ะ with its dropped subject. The closing dialogue drills register at a street stall per the soft-wrong rule: เอาข้าว is understood-but-blunt, the other distractors fail on meaning and situation. All per-syllable tones verify against each token's vocab toneReason via wordId.

exercises.title: You already own these sentences
body: Every sentence in this unit recombines words you've already learned — nothing new to memorize, only new combinations.
Thai needs no 'is': the describing word follows straight after the thing. ข้าวอร่อยมาก — 'rice delicious very' — is a complete, natural sentence.
Same trick everywhere: อันนี้แพงมาก 'this one is really expensive', วันนี้ร้อนมาก 'today is very hot'. And วันนี้ itself is just วัน + นี้ — 'day-this'.
exercises.thaiExample: ข้าวอร่อยมาก

choices.meaning: To be hungry (lit. hungry for rice)

exercises.explanation: Three near-openers on the same ข้าว: กิน stays mid, หิว and ขอ both rise — the consonant and tone of the first syllable decide whether you're eating, hungry, or asking.
Noun first, then the describing words pile on after: ข้าว + อร่อย + มาก. No 'is' anywhere — and question-particle คะ has no place in a plain statement.
อันนี้ + adjective + มาก. ถูก would make a fine sentence too — but it says this one is cheap, the opposite of the meaning. หิว is a feeling; things aren't hungry.

exercises.meaning: Today is very hot — literally 'day-this hot very'.
exercises.explanation: วัน + นี้ = 'day-this' = today, composed from two words you know. วันไหน would ask 'which day?' — a question this statement isn't asking.
ผม + หิว + มาก — feeling sentences work exactly like adjective sentences. ค่ะ can't close this one: ผม is a man talking, ค่ะ is the female particle.

exercises.title: Chain the verbs
body: Thai chains verbs with nothing between them: ไป + กิน + ข้าว — 'go eat rice'. No 'to', no 'and', no endings.
Answering ไปไหน ('where are you going?') with ไปกินข้าว is one of the most natural exchanges in Thailand — you'll build exactly that in the next lesson.

exercises.meaning: Off to eat. / I'm going to go eat.
exercises.explanation: ไป then กิน, verb after verb — Thai never says 'to'. มาจาก points the wrong way: it means 'come from' and can't chain onto ไป.

exercises.thai: เหนื่อยมากค่ะ

choices.thai: เหนื่อยมากค่ะ
ร้อนมากค่ะ

exercises.explanation: No 'I' anywhere — Thai states the feeling bare and lets context point it. The ค่ะ tail tells you a woman is speaking.

exercises.meaning: Could I get some water, please? (male speaker)
exercises.explanation: A man closes the request with ครับ. ค่ะ and คะ belong to female speakers — and high-tone คะ only ever ends questions.

exercises.context: Evening at a street-food stall. The vendor looks up as you walk over. You're a man, and you want rice.
exercises.question: How do you reply?

choices.thai: ขอข้าวหน่อยครับ
choices.note: ขอ + thing + หน่อย + ครับ — the polite request frame from Ordering Food, aimed at exactly this moment.
You'd get your rice — but bare เอา with no particle is how you talk to a friend holding the pot, not a vendor you've just met. ครับ at minimum.

choices.thai: ข้าวอร่อยครับ
choices.note: Perfectly polite — but it means “the rice is delicious.” That's a compliment after eating, not an answer to “what'll you have?”

choices.thai: ไม่เป็นไรครับ
choices.note: ไม่เป็นไร waves off an apology or a thank-you. Nobody apologized — the vendor asked for your order.
